TSLA: Lemonade and Tesla Collaborate: Innovative Car Insurance Shakes Industry. Lemonade Inc. and Tesla have partnered to introduce innovative car insurance for Tesla's Full Self-Driving vehicles, offering 50% lower rates for FSD miles and leveraging crucial Tesla data for precision. This collaboration has boosted investor confidence, leading to a 15.38% increase in Lemonade Inc. stocks and higher price targets from analysts who recognize Lemonade's digital strategies as a significant advantage over traditional insurers. Despite some financial deficits and property pricing trends, the company is poised for growth in casualty lines, with current revenue projections exceeding $526.5 million.

Toyota and Tesla Want You to Forget About the All-Electric RAV4 They Built Together. This article details the largely forgotten partnership between Toyota and Tesla in producing the all-electric RAV4 between 2012 and 2014. It highlights Toyota's initial reluctance towards EVs, their earlier 90s electric RAV4, and how the 2012 model utilized Tesla's advanced battery and motor technology. Despite combining Toyota's manufacturing expertise with Tesla's EV innovation, the partnership ended as their core philosophies diverged and Toyota reportedly lost money on each unit sold, making the RAV4 EV a rare and unsupported collector's item today.

Some intelligent driving concept stocks rose, with Pony AI and WeRide experiencing significant intraday gains.. Pony AI and WeRide, along with other intelligent driving concept stocks, saw significant intraday gains. This surge follows Elon Musk's announcement that Tesla's Robotaxi service is expected to be widely deployed in the US this year. Securities firms like Dongwu Securities and Ping An Securities are optimistic about the future of autonomous driving, foreseeing commercialization and overseas expansion.

Why Lemonade (LMND) Stock Is Trading Up Today. Shares of Lemonade (LMND) surged by 13.1% after the company unveiled a new insurance product for Tesla vehicles equipped with Full Self-Driving (FSD) technology, offering approximately 50% lower per-mile rates. This offering resulted from a partnership with Tesla, providing Lemonade with vehicle data that indicated FSD-driven Teslas had significantly fewer accidents. The stock's rise, which also marked a new 52-week high, was supported by recent positive analyst ratings and strong third-quarter 2025 financial results.

Form 424B2 BANK OF NOVA SCOTIA. This SEC filing (Form 424B2) from The Bank of Nova Scotia details the terms of new Market Linked Securities. These auto-callable securities offer a contingent coupon payment with a memory feature and contingent downside principal at risk, linked to the performance of common stocks of Broadcom Inc., Meta Platforms, Inc., Shopify Inc., and Tesla, Inc., due January 25, 2029. The document outlines the mechanics of contingent coupon payments, automatic call conditions, potential loss of principal, and various risk considerations for investors, emphasizing that returns depend on the lowest performing underlying stock.

Tesla launches robotaxi rides in Austin with no human safety driver. Tesla has launched robotaxi services in Austin, Texas, allowing passengers to ride without a human safety driver. CEO Elon Musk announced the milestone on Thursday, noting that the company is starting with a mix of unsupervised and supervised vehicles, with the ratio of fully driverless cars expected to increase over time. This development follows initial limited deployments and testing phases that began in June and December of the previous year.

Tesla Starts Public Robotaxi Rides in Austin With Ex-Employee. Tesla has commenced public robotaxi rides in Austin, Texas, with its first customer experiencing a ride without any human intervention in the front seats. This marks a significant milestone for the company, which has been conducting human-supervised rides for several months. CEO Elon Musk highlighted this progress on X, sharing a video from a former Tesla AI engineer who was reportedly the first public user.

Tesla starts robotaxi rides without safety monitors in Austin. Tesla has launched robotaxi services in Austin, Texas, operating without human safety monitors, as announced by CEO Elon Musk. This move signifies a progression in the company's autonomous driving technology. Concurrently, Musk anticipates regulatory approval for Tesla's Full Self-Driving system in Europe and China as early as next month.

Lemonade’s Tesla insurance product validates FSD technology, Morgan Stanley notes. Morgan Stanley reiterated an Equalweight rating and $425.00 price target for Tesla, noting that Lemonade's new autonomous car insurance product validates Tesla's Full Self-Driving (FSD) technology. Lemonade's policy ties insurance pricing to FSD miles driven, offering up to 50% rate reductions, which incentivizes FSD usage and signals the insurer's recognition of improved accident risk during autonomous operation. This development suggests that insurance could become a market-based validator for autonomous driving advancements.

Sam Altman Slams Elon Musk, Tesla Over Autopilot Crash Deaths, Questions Self-Driving Safety As Feud Heats Up: 'Far From A Safe Thing…'. OpenAI co-founder Sam Altman criticized Elon Musk and Tesla over Autopilot-related accident fatalities, escalating their ongoing feud. Altman highlighted that over 50 people have reportedly died in Autopilot-related crashes, calling the system "far from a safe thing to have released." He also dismissed Musk's shifting complaints about ChatGPT, stating that OpenAI is continually working to ensure user safety and benefit while Musk has called the model both "too restrictive" and "too relaxed."

Elon Musk says Tesla will likely sell humanoid robots by end of next year. Elon Musk announced that Tesla plans to sell its Optimus humanoid robots to the public by the end of 2027, after deploying them for more complex tasks in Tesla factories this year. He envisions a future where billions of these robots assist with household duties like childcare and elder care. Despite challenges in ramping up production for new technologies like Optimus and Cybercab, Musk believes the initial slow production will eventually become "insanely fast."

Davos-Musk expects Europe, China to approve Tesla's FSD system next month. Elon Musk anticipates that Tesla's Full Self-Driving (FSD) system will receive approval in Europe and China by February, which would significantly boost the company's revenue from software and services outside the U.S. This announcement, made at the World Economic Forum in Davos, caused Tesla's shares to rise by 1.5%. The FSD system is classified as an advanced driver assistance feature and its deployment has faced scrutiny from regulators over safety concerns, particularly in Europe, where regulations are tougher.
TGT: Target Corporation Declares Regular Quarterly Dividend. Target Corporation's board of directors has declared a quarterly dividend of $1.14 per common share. This dividend is payable on March 1, 2026, to shareholders of record as of February 11, 2026, marking the company's 234th consecutive dividend payment. Target, headquartered in Minneapolis, operates nearly 2,000 stores and Target.com, and has a long history of community giving.

Target Stacks Its Board With Star Executives From Nike, HanesBrands. Target Corporation has appointed John Hoke, III, a longtime Nike design leader, and Steve Bratspies, former CEO of HanesBrands, to its board of directors. These appointments are part of Target's strategy to enhance innovation and increase shareholder value as it enters a new growth phase with CEO Michael Fiddelke. The company also declared a quarterly dividend of $1.14 per common share, payable on March 1, 2026.

1.5 million bottles of stain remover sold at Walmart, Target recalled for potential serious bacteria exposure. Over 1.5 million bottles of Angry Orange Enzyme Stain Remover, sold at major retailers like Walmart and Target, have been recalled due to potential contamination with Pseudomonas aeruginosa bacteria. While typically harmless to healthy individuals, this bacteria can cause serious infections in those with weakened immune systems or certain medical conditions. Consumers are advised to immediately stop using the product and contact Thrasio for a full refund by emailing a photo of the marked bottle.

1.5 million bottles of stain remover sold at Walmart, Target recalled for potential serious bacteria exposure. Over 1.5 million bottles of Angry Orange Enzyme Stain Remover have been recalled from major retailers like Walmart and Target due to potential contamination with Pseudomonas aeruginosa bacteria. While no illnesses have been reported, consumers are advised to stop using the product immediately and contact Thrasio for a full refund by emailing a photo of the marked bottle. This bacteria can pose a serious risk to individuals with weakened immune systems or underlying health conditions if inhaled or absorbed through broken skin.

Target names John R. Hoke III and Stephen B. Bratspies to board of directors. Target Corporation has announced the appointment of John R. Hoke III and Stephen B. Bratspies to its board of directors, effective March 1, 2026, and April 1, 2026, respectively. Hoke, formerly of NIKE, Inc., will serve on the Compensation & Human Capital Management and Governance & Sustainability Committees, bringing innovation and design expertise. Bratspies, previously CEO of HanesBrands Inc. and Chief Merchandising Officer at Walmart Inc., will join the Audit & Risk and Infrastructure & Finance Committees.

1.5 million bottles of stain remover sold at Walmart, Target recalled for potential serious bacteria exposure. Over 1 million bottles of Angry Orange Enzyme Stain Remover, sold at major retailers including Walmart and Target, have been recalled due to potential contamination with Pseudomonas aeruginosa bacteria. This bacteria can cause serious infections in individuals with weakened immune systems, external medical devices, or underlying lung conditions. Consumers are advised to stop using the product immediately and contact Thrasio for a full refund by emailing a photo of the marked bottle to productrecall@angryorange.com.

Target appoints former Nike, HanesBrands executives to board. Target Corporation has appointed John Hoke, former chief innovation officer at Nike, and Steve Bratspies, former CEO of HanesBrands, to its board of directors. These appointments are part of Target's preparation for a leadership transition under incoming CEO Michael Fiddelke and aim to strengthen governance and long-term value creation. The company's stock is currently trading below its InvestingPro Fair Value, suggesting potential for investors.

Target Recruits Nike And Walmart Talent Before CEO Handoff. Target has brought in two new board directors, one from Nike and one from Walmart, ahead of COO Michael Fiddelke taking over as CEO in February. These hires signal Target's focus on improving product quality, value, and operational execution to combat declining sales and investor pressure. The company aims to address three consecutive quarters of falling comparable sales and a 22% stock drop over the past year.

1.5 million bottles of stain remover sold at Walmart, Target recalled for potential serious bacteria exposure. Over 1 million bottles of Angry Orange Enzyme Stain Remover sold at major retailers like Walmart and Target have been recalled due to potential bacterial contamination. The bacteria, Pseudomonas aeruginosa, can cause serious infections, especially in individuals with weakened immune systems or underlying health conditions. Consumers are advised to stop using the product immediately and contact Thrasio for a full refund by submitting a photo of the marked bottle.

Two big-brand bosses join Target’s board as new CEO takes over. Target Corporation has appointed John Hoke, former chief innovation officer at Nike, and Steve Bratspies, former CEO of HanesBrands, to its Board of Directors, effective March 1 and April 1, 2026, respectively. These appointments aim to strengthen the board with expertise in design, merchandising, and operations as the company prepares for its next growth phase under incoming CEO Michael Fiddelke. Hoke will serve on the Governance & Sustainability and Compensation & Human Capital Management committees, while Bratspies will join the Audit & Risk and Infrastructure & Finance committees.

Target Appoints Former Nike and HanesBrands Executives to Board of Directors. Target Corporation has announced the election of John Hoke, III, former chief innovation officer at NIKE, Inc., and Steve Bratspies, former CEO of HanesBrands, Inc., to its Board of Directors. These appointments aim to strengthen the Board's governance and support Target's strategy for its next chapter of growth under incoming CEO Michael Fiddelke, aligning with the company's focus on style, design, and value. Hoke brings extensive experience in design-led brand building from Nike, while Bratspies offers a strong background in leading complex consumer businesses, merchandising, and operations from HanesBrands and Walmart.
SBUX: Starbucks Stock Brews Up Third Straight Weekly Gain: Will 2026 End A 4-Year Losing Streak?. Starbucks (SBUX) is on track for its third consecutive weekly gain, with shares up nearly 14% in 2026, marking its strongest start to a year in five years. Analysts are becoming more optimistic, highlighted by a William Blair upgrade to 'Outperform' and a Bank of America price target increase, anticipating a rebound in U.S. comparable sales. However, retail traders remain largely skeptical leading up to the coffee giant's Q1 earnings report next week.

Starbucks Stock Brews Up Third Straight Weekly Gain: Will 2026 End A 4-Year Losing Streak?. Starbucks (SBUX) is on track for its third consecutive weekly gain, with shares up nearly 14% so far in 2026, driven by a more positive Wall Street sentiment ahead of its upcoming Q1 earnings. Analysts, including William Blair and Bank of America, have upgraded their outlooks or price targets, anticipating a rebound in domestic comparable sales for the coffee giant. Despite Wall Street's optimism regarding CEO Brian Niccol's turnaround efforts, retail traders on Stocktwits remain largely "bearish" heading into the earnings report.

Starbucks to lay out long-term growth plan at Jan. 29 investor event. Starbucks Coffee Company (NASDAQ: SBUX) has announced its 2026 Investor Day will take place on Thursday, January 29, 2026, where the company will unveil its long-term growth strategy. The event will feature presentations and a Q&A session with executive leadership, including CEO Brian Niccol and CFO Cathy Smith. A live webcast will be available for all interested parties from 8:00 a.m. ET to 12:00 p.m. ET on the company's Investor Relations website.
DIS: Fox Corp. (Class A): How a Legacy Media Stock Is Rebuilding Its Flagship for the Streaming Era. Fox Corp. (Class A) is strategically focusing on live sports, news, and ad-driven entertainment, primarily in the U.S., setting it apart from rivals that chase global subscriber counts or debt-heavy streaming empires. The company leverages its strong positions in Fox Sports and Fox News as profit centers, while Tubi provides a scalable ad-supported streaming channel for digital distribution. Fox Corp. positions itself as a financially disciplined alternative, offering investors exposure to resilient live content and advertising with a leaner balance sheet post-Disney asset sale.
